description: Verified GGUF/GGML model catalog for llama.cpp. 65 models (purged hallucinated entries; preflight HEAD-probes every URL before download).

capabilityFlags.isCoding: Model is trained/optimized for code generation, completion, and debugging
capabilityFlags.isReasoning: Model supports chain-of-thought, step-by-step logical reasoning
capabilityFlags.isVoice: Model supports voice/audio input or output (speech-to-text, TTS)
capabilityFlags.isWriting: Model is good at creative writing, long-form content, essays, documentation
capabilityFlags.isMultilingual: Model supports multiple human languages (not just English)
capabilityFlags.isChat: Model is optimized for conversational/chat interactions
capabilityFlags.leaderboardRank: Position on OpenRouter LLM Leaderboard (Nov 2025) if applicable. Open-weight models only.

models.bestFor: Fast lightweight coding assistant. Good for Python, TypeScript, and quick code tasks on low-RAM machines.
models.notes: Smallest Qwen coder. 32K context window. Great speed-to-quality ratio for simple coding tasks.

models.bestFor: Enterprise-grade code generation. Top-tier for Python, TypeScript, Rust, Go. Rivals GPT-4 on coding benchmarks at 14B.
models.notes: ★ RECOMMENDED for Claude Code. Best coding-to-RAM ratio in the collection. Supports 92+ programming languages.

models.bestFor: Strong reasoning distilled from DeepSeek R1 into Qwen 14B. Best mid-size reasoner — beats many 30B+ models on math and logic benchmarks.
models.notes: Most popular DeepSeek R1 distill variant. 23.9K+ HuggingFace runs. Chain-of-thought reasoning. Excellent coding and math. Only needs 12GB RAM.

models.bestFor: Microsoft's best small model. 84.8% MMLU — massive jump over Phi-3. Strong at math, coding, and structured reasoning in only 9 GB.
models.notes: Trained on high-quality synthetic + organic data. Huge improvement over Phi-3 series. 16K context. Excellent efficiency for 12-16GB RAM setups.

models.bestFor: Deep reasoning variant of Phi-4. Chain-of-thought structured thinking. Excellent at math competition problems, logic puzzles, and complex debugging.
models.notes: Phi-4 fine-tuned for systematic reasoning. Uses structured Thought/Solution format. Slower than base Phi-4 due to reasoning overhead but significantly more accurate on hard problems.

models.bestFor: Higher quality everyday coder. ~3% better than Q4_K_M for only +2 GB. Best upgrade for daily coding with spare RAM.
models.notes: Q5_K_M of the recommended coding model. 92+ languages. Noticeable quality improvement on complex code generation.

models.bestFor: Near-lossless everyday coder. ~97% of F16 quality. Best for when you want maximum Qwen 14B fidelity.
models.notes: Q6_K quantization. Near-original quality with meaningful size savings vs Q8_0. Sweet spot for quality purists.

models.bestFor: Top-tier agentic coding. SWE-bench leader. Complex refactoring, multi-file edits, architecture.
models.notes: ★ BEST open-source coding model 2025/2026. #1 on SWE-bench. Designed for agentic coding workflows.
models.manualReason: gated repo (HTTP 401) -- needs HF auth token; populate sha256 manually after `huggingface-cli login`

models.bestFor: Maximum quality 14B coder. ~99% of original F16. Best possible Qwen 14B output on a 64GB machine.
models.notes: Q8_0 quantization — virtually lossless. 15.7 GB is affordable on 64GB. The ultimate 14B coding experience.

models.bestFor: Higher quality #1 coding model. ~3% better output quality than Q4_K_M at +2.5 GB cost. Worth it with 64GB RAM.
models.notes: Q5_K_M quantization of SWE-bench #1 coder. Same model, better precision. Ideal upgrade for 64GB systems.
models.manualReason: gated repo (HTTP 401) -- needs HF auth token; populate sha256 manually after `huggingface-cli login`

models.bestFor: Top MoE reasoning model. Only 3B active params = fast inference from 30B knowledge. Hybrid thinking mode. Excellent for coding agents and complex reasoning.
models.notes: ★ MoE architecture: 30B total, 3B active per token. Supports /think and /no_think toggles. Strong on MMLU-PRO. Apache 2.0 licensed. One of the best models for 24-32GB RAM setups.

models.bestFor: Near-lossless #1 coding model. ~97% of original F16 quality. Maximum coding fidelity for complex refactoring.
models.notes: Q6_K quantization — near-lossless. Best choice when you want maximum Devstral quality and have 24GB+ free RAM.
models.manualReason: gated repo (HTTP 401) -- needs HF auth token; populate sha256 manually after `huggingface-cli login`

models.bestFor: Ultra-fast speech-to-text. Multilingual. Ideal for real-time transcription on any hardware.
models.notes: Smallest Whisper model. 75 MB. Runs on any machine. Good enough for meeting notes and quick transcription.

models.bestFor: Balanced speech-to-text. Better accuracy than Tiny with minimal extra cost.
models.notes: 142 MB. Good balance of speed and accuracy for everyday transcription.

models.bestFor: High quality transcription. Good for professional meeting notes and podcast transcription.
models.notes: 466 MB. Noticeably better accuracy than Base. Recommended for serious transcription work.

models.bestFor: Testing pipelines, edge deployment, and smoke testing Ollama setup. Not suitable for real coding or reasoning.
models.notes: Smallest usable model. Perfect for verifying your Ollama/Claude Code pipeline works before loading a large model.

models.bestFor: Pipeline testing and edge devices. Fastest possible inference. Not for production coding.
models.notes: Trained on 3 trillion tokens despite tiny size. Good for testing your setup works before loading bigger models.

models.bestFor: Ultra-tiny code completion. Only 800 MB. Pipeline testing, edge devices, code autocomplete.
models.notes: Google's smallest coding model. Full Q8 precision at under 1 GB. Great for smoke testing and autocomplete.
models.manualReason: gated repo (HTTP 401) -- needs HF auth token; populate sha256 manually after `huggingface-cli login`

models.bestFor: Ultra-fast code completion on low-end hardware. Only 1.1 GB. Good for autocomplete and simple coding tasks.
models.notes: Smallest dedicated coding model. Just over 1 GB. Perfect for edge devices or as a fast code autocomplete engine.

models.bestFor: Professional transcription quality. Multilingual. Near-large accuracy at half the size.
models.notes: 1.5 GB. High accuracy for professional work. Good multilingual support.

models.bestFor: Ultra-lightweight tasks on low-end hardware. Quick prototyping, simple code generation, basic Q&A.
models.notes: Surprisingly capable for its size. Runs on almost any machine with 4GB+ RAM. 4K context window.

models.bestFor: Lightweight reasoning + coding on low-end hardware. Hybrid thinking mode with /think and /no_think toggles.
models.notes: Official Qwen3 4B. Supports thinking mode (chain-of-thought) and non-thinking mode. 32K context. Only 2.5 GB.

models.bestFor: Maximum transcription accuracy. Best for professional/broadcast transcription, difficult audio, and multilingual content.
models.notes: 2.9 GB. Best Whisper model. Use when accuracy matters more than speed.

models.bestFor: Fastest coding assistant for low-RAM setups. Slight quality tradeoff vs Q5 variant.
models.notes: Smaller quantization for machines with limited RAM. Still decent for straightforward coding tasks.

models.bestFor: General-purpose assistant — chat, light coding, summarization, translation, and writing.
models.notes: Excellent general model. Sliding window attention for efficient long-context handling.

models.bestFor: Google's dedicated coding model. Good at Python, JS, Java, Go. Strong code infilling support.
models.notes: Trained on 500B+ code tokens. Supports fill-in-the-middle (FIM). Good alternative to DeepSeek Coder at 7B size.

models.bestFor: Fast code completion and generation for common languages (Python, JS, Java, C++). Good balance of speed and quality.
models.notes: Higher quantization (Q5) gives better output quality than Q4 variant. Great for quick coding tasks.

models.bestFor: Enterprise coding tasks. 116 programming languages. Good for regulated environments.
models.notes: IBM's enterprise-grade coding model. 4K context. Trained on permissively licensed code. ~5GB RAM.
models.manualReason: URL not found (HTTP 404) -- catalog entry may be hallucinated or repo renamed; verify huggingfacePage and update downloadUrl

models.bestFor: Compact multi-language coder. Good for everyday coding tasks when RAM is limited.
models.notes: Lighter StarCoder2. 600+ languages. ~5GB RAM. Good speed-to-quality ratio for code completion.

models.bestFor: Fast coding assistant with good quality. Best choice when you need speed over maximum accuracy.
models.notes: Lighter sibling of the 14B coder. Great speed-to-quality ratio. Use this for everyday coding, 14B for complex tasks.

models.bestFor: Advanced reasoning with step-by-step thinking. Math, logic puzzles, complex debugging, and analytical tasks.
models.notes: DeepSeek's reinforcement-learned reasoning model. Shows chain-of-thought process. Strong at math and logic.
models.manualReason: gated repo (HTTP 401) -- needs HF auth token; populate sha256 manually after `huggingface-cli login`

models.bestFor: Same reasoning approach as R1-70B but fits in 8GB RAM. Best value reasoning model in the collection.
models.notes: Distilled from the 70B R1 model into Llama 8B architecture. Retains much of the reasoning ability at fraction of the size.

models.bestFor: Chain-of-thought reasoning tasks, problem solving, unrestricted analysis, and creative writing.
models.notes: Community fine-tune with enhanced reasoning capabilities. 'Thinking' mode shows step-by-step logic. Uncensored = no content filtering.

models.bestFor: Creative writing, roleplay, and conversational tasks. Full F16 precision gives maximum quality for this model size.
models.notes: Community fine-tune of Phi-3.5 Mini. F16 = no quantization loss but uses 3x more RAM than Q4.
models.manualReason: gated repo (HTTP 401) -- needs HF auth token; populate sha256 manually after `huggingface-cli login`

models.bestFor: Code generation, infilling, and instruction following. Strong at Python, C++, Java, and bash scripting.
models.notes: Meta's dedicated coding model. 13B offers good quality-to-speed ratio. Supports code infilling (fill-in-the-middle).

models.bestFor: Balanced coding + reasoning at 14B. Good general-purpose model from Microsoft.
models.notes: Microsoft 14B. Stronger than Phi-3 Mini across all tasks. 4K context. ~9GB RAM.

models.bestFor: Best general-purpose model in the collection. Chat, coding, analysis, translation, writing — does everything well at 14B.
models.notes: Qwen 2.5 series excels at multilingual tasks and structured output. Strong at following complex instructions.
models.manualReason: URL not found (HTTP 404) -- catalog entry may be hallucinated or repo renamed; verify huggingfacePage and update downloadUrl

models.bestFor: Multi-language code generation. Trained on 600+ programming languages. Good for polyglot projects.
models.notes: BigCode StarCoder2 15B. Trained on The Stack v2. Strong at code completion and infilling across many languages.

models.bestFor: Large-scale enterprise coding. 116 languages. Stronger quality than 8B variant.
models.notes: IBM's larger enterprise coding model. 8K context. ~12GB RAM. Good for complex enterprise codebases.

models.bestFor: Professional multi-language code generation, refactoring, and code review. Trained on 80+ programming languages.
models.notes: One of the strongest open-source coding models. Excellent at code completion, debugging, and explaining code.
models.manualReason: gated repo (HTTP 401) -- needs HF auth token; populate sha256 manually after `huggingface-cli login`

models.bestFor: Strong all-rounder with vision support. Coding, reasoning, multilingual chat (24 languages), and image understanding.
models.notes: Mistral's latest Small model. Vision-capable via mmproj. Apache 2.0 licensed. Rivals Devstral at same parameter count but adds vision + chat.

models.bestFor: Strong all-rounder with vision. Coding, reasoning, creative writing, multilingual chat, and image understanding.
models.notes: Google's latest Gemma 3 27B. Vision via mmproj file. Competitive with Mistral Small 3.1 and Devstral at similar size.

models.bestFor: Maximum open-source coding quality. Complex architecture, system design, multi-language projects.
models.notes: Largest Qwen coder. Matches GPT-4 on HumanEval. Needs significant RAM but delivers exceptional code quality.

models.bestFor: Large-scale code generation. Strong at Python, C++, Java, bash. Meta's biggest dedicated coder.
models.notes: Meta Code Llama 34B. Solid general-purpose coding. Needs 24GB+ RAM. Superseded by newer models but still capable.

models.bestFor: Higher quality max coder. Best dense coding model with improved quantization. Fits comfortably in 64GB.
models.notes: Q5_K_M of the largest Qwen coder. GPT-4 level on HumanEval with better precision than Q4_K_M.

models.bestFor: Maximum reasoning power. Complex architecture decisions, deep analysis, research-level problem solving, and technical writing.
models.notes: ⚠️ Requires 48GB+ RAM (or GPU offload). Extremely powerful but slow. Use only when you need the absolute best reasoning quality.

models.bestFor: Meta's dense flagship. 128K context. Exceptional at coding, reasoning, multilingual chat, and complex instruction following. The gold standard for 70B-class models.
models.notes: ⚠️ Requires 48GB+ RAM. 42.5 GB file. Dense 70B — all params active every token. Supports tool use and function calling. 8 languages. Best run with GPU offloading on 64GB systems.

models.bestFor: Meta's flagship MoE model. Strong multilingual coding, reasoning, and multimodal understanding. 16 experts, 17B active.
models.notes: ⚠️ Requires 72GB+ RAM. Split into 2 GGUF files (49.8 GB + 15.5 GB). 12 languages supported. MoE architecture.

models.bestFor: Ultra-fast chat on minimal hardware. Google's tiniest Gemma 3 model, ideal for edge devices and low-RAM systems.
models.notes: 1B params, 32K context. Great for simple chat, summarization, translation on devices with 2-3 GB RAM.

models.bestFor: Best-in-class 4B model. Strong reasoning, multilingual, and coding for its size. Runs on 4 GB RAM.
models.notes: Google's 4B Gemma 3. Multimodal capable (vision). 128K context. Excellent quality-to-size ratio. Outperforms many 7B models.

models.bestFor: Ultra-lightweight Meta model. Fastest inference, ideal for edge deployment, basic chat, and text extraction.
models.notes: Meta's tiniest Llama. 128K context. Supports tool calling. Multilingual (8 languages). Under 1 GB download.

models.bestFor: Meta's sweet spot for mobile and edge. Great for summarization, tool use, and multilingual chat at minimal RAM.
models.notes: 128K context. Supports tool calling and structured output. 8 languages. Excellent on phones, tablets, and Raspberry Pi.

models.bestFor: HuggingFace's purpose-built tiny model. Strong text rewriting, summarization, and basic code on ultra-low RAM.
models.notes: 1.7B params trained on high-quality data. Beats many 3B models on benchmarks. 8K context. Very fast inference.

models.bestFor: Best small reasoning model. Beats Phi-3 Mini and many 7B models. Excellent for math, coding, and structured output.
models.notes: Microsoft's latest mini. 128K context. Tool calling support. State-of-the-art for sub-4B reasoning. Replaces Phi-3 Mini.

models.bestFor: Enterprise-grade tiny model. Good for RAG, summarization, tool use, and code on minimal hardware.
models.notes: IBM's 2B enterprise model. 128K context. Function calling. Trained for safety and enterprise compliance.

models.bestFor: Enterprise-grade 8B model. Strong RAG, function calling, coding, and document analysis.
models.notes: IBM's 8B enterprise model. 128K context. Guardian safety models available. Competitive with Llama 3.1 8B.

models.bestFor: Tiny reasoning model. Supports thinking mode with <think> tags. Strong multilingual chat at ultra-low RAM.
models.notes: 1.7B with dual-mode (thinking on/off). 32K context. 119 languages. Punches above its weight in reasoning tasks.

models.bestFor: Specialized function-calling model. Best for building AI agents, tool use, and structured JSON output.
models.notes: Fine-tuned Llama 3.1 8B for function calling. Supports parallel function calls. Ideal for agentic workflows.

models.bestFor: Google's strongest mid-size Gemma. Multimodal (vision + text). Excellent for coding, reasoning, and creative writing.
models.notes: 12B params, 128K context. Native vision support. Outperforms many 14B models. ShieldGemma safety built-in.

models.bestFor: OpenRouter #1 ranked open model (Nov 2025, 8.21T tokens, +231% growth). Strong reasoning + coding from Xiaomi.
models.notes: Closest local equivalent to MiMo-V2-Pro. Hybrid thinking model. 256K context.
models.manualReason: URL not found (HTTP 404) -- catalog entry may be hallucinated or repo renamed; verify huggingfacePage and update downloadUrl

models.bestFor: OpenRouter #3 (5.41T tokens). Datacenter-class MoE. Best open reasoning model.
models.notes: DATACENTER ONLY. 671B params total, 37B active per token. Multi-part GGUF. Use Unsloth Dynamic for 250GB Q2.

models.bestFor: OpenRouter #5 (4.75T tokens, +556% growth). Strong agentic + tool use.
models.notes: MoE architecture. Multi-part GGUF. Workstation/server only.

models.bestFor: Successor to M2 with refined reasoning. OpenRouter #8 family member.
models.notes: Latest MiniMax. Hybrid thinking. Multi-part GGUF.

models.bestFor: OpenRouter #9 (2.81T tokens). Multimodal (vision+text). Free tier on OpenRouter.
models.notes: Vision-language model. Q8_0 official release from StepFun.

models.bestFor: OpenRouter #17 (1.88T tokens). OpenAI's first open-weight model since GPT-2. Strong reasoning.
models.notes: MXFP4 native quant. Multi-part GGUF. Workstation class. Apache 2.0.
